A Visit to the 	National War 	12	Memorial	SDG Promotes respect for peace, national 16 service and responsible citizenship. Wonder WindowHomage means ‘something that is said or Celebrating their Honouring their done to show respect publicly for someone’. Victories Sacrifices Whom should every Indian pay homage to? visiting a war Complete the table by writing any three memorial ways in which we can celebrate the victories and honour the sacrifices of the soldiers. An example has been done for you. Share your answers with your classmates and the teacher.Peek InsideA girl writes a letter to her friend to share her thoughts after visiting the National War Memorial in New Delhi. The visit helps her understand the courage and sacrifices of Indian soldiers. Let’s read to discover how this memorial honours the brave heroes of our nation.House No. 77/1Laxmi NagarDelhi24 October 20XXDear Ashi,I hope you and your family are doing well.Do you remember our discussion about wars and why peace is so important? We spoke about India’s long struggle to gain independence from British rule. Even after becoming independent, our nation has had to fight several battles to safeguard1 its borders, protect its unity, and ensure the safety of its citizens. Thinking about this made me curious about the brave soldiers who defended our country. They gave their lives so that we could live peacefully today. Who were these heroes? Where did they come 1. safeguard: to protect from? Does anyone remember their stories?	from harm or dangerLast week, during a school educational trip to New Delhi, I discovered answers to many of these questions. You might remember how excited I was about visiting places of historical and educational significance2. The trip turned out to be very informative, and I learned many fascinating things that I would love to share with you.Let me begin by asking you to guess the monument that impressed me the most. See if you can figure it out. The construction of this monument started in April 2018 and was completed in February 2019. It was conceived3 and later inaugurated by the 2. significance: importance Prime Minister of India, Shri Narendra Modi in February 2019. 3. conceived: plannedIt covers nearly 40 acres of land close to the famous India Gate in New Delhi. More than just its size or location, the monument stands as a tribute4 to the courage and sacrifice of India’s brave soldiers. By now you must have guessed the name of this place — the National War Memorial5. It truly fulfils a long-cherished6 dream of honouring our fallen heroes. When we visited the memorial, our teacher explained that even after gaining independence, India has had to face several wars to defend its borders and protect its citizens. The peace and safety we enjoy today are possible because countless men and women have served in the Indian Armed Forces with dedication. Sadly, war often demands great sacrifice, and many soldiers have laid down their lives for the country even after independence in 1947.This is why memorials like the National War Memorial are so significant. They remind us of the true price of freedom and ensure that the sacrifices of our soldiers are never forgotten. We also learned that each stone block in the memorial is called a tablet. These tablets carry the names of soldiers who gave their lives tribute: something said or done to show respectmemorial: a structure or place built to remember people or eventscherished: deeply valued or lovedWhy is it important for a nation to remember the names of those who sacrificed their lives?while defending India in different wars ?since 1947. These include the Indo-China conflict of 1962, the Indo-Pakistan wars of 1965 and 1971, and the Kargil War of 1999. The memorial contains about 29,000 tablets, on which the names of more than 26,000 martyrs7 are engraved.It was inspiring to learn that the nation honours the bravery of its soldiers through various awards. Our teacher spoke about the Param Vir Chakra (PVC), which is India’s highest gallantry8 award during wartime. So far, 21 soldiers have received this honour. She also explained other important awards such as the Maha Vir Chakra (MVC), Kirti Chakra (KC), Vir Chakra (VrC), and Shaurya Chakra (SC).Major Somnath Sharma became the first recipient of the Param Vir Chakra. He was awarded this honour posthumously9 for his bravery in the Battle of Badgam in 1947. Later, other soldiers were awarded the PVC for their exceptional courage during the India-China war of 1962 and the India-Pakistan war of 1965. One PVC was also awarded for outstanding service during the United Nations Peacekeeping mission in Congo.	7. martyrs: people who die for a great causeDuring the 1971 war, several brave soldiers 8. gallantry: brave and noble behaviour received the Param Vir Chakra for their remarkable 9. posthumously: given after a person’s death heroism. Among them were Lance Naik Albert Ekka, Flying Officer Nirmal Jit Singh Sekhon, Second Lieutenant Arun Khetarpal, and Major Hoshiar Singh.Some Navy personnel Captain Mahendra Nath Mulla was also honoured with the Maha Vir Chakra (MVC) for displaying remarkable courage and leadership.Our teacher then read out the citation10 of Param Vir Chakra recipient Major Somnath Sharma. Listening to it deeply moved me and filled me with inspiration. His bravery left all of us amazed and respectful. I felt proud of such heroes and hope to show the 10. citation: an official statement praising same courage one day when I serve the nation by someone’s achievement or bravery joining the army.CITATIONMAJOR SOMNATH SHARMA, (IC-521),  4th Battalion, Kumaon RegimentOn 3 November 1947, Major Somnath Sharma's company was ordered on a fighting patrol to Badgam in the Kashmir Valley . He reached his objective at first light on 3 November and took up a position south of Badgam at 11:00 hours. The enemy, estimated at about 500 attacked his company position from three sides; the company began to sustain heavy casualties. Fully realizing the gravity of the situation and the direct threat that would result to both the aerodrome and Srinagar via Hum Hom, Major Somnath Sharma urged his company to fight the enemy tenaciously. With extreme bravery he kept rushing across the open ground to his sections exposing himself to heavy and accurate fire to urge them to hold on. Keeping his nerve, he skilfully directed the fire of his sections into the everadvancing enemy. He repeatedly exposed himself to the full fury of enemy fire and laid out cloth strips to guide our aircraft onto their targets in full view of the enemy. Realising that casualties had affected the effectiveness of his light automatics, this officer whose left hand was in plaster, personally commenced filling magazines and issuing them to the light machine gunners. A mortar shell landed right in the middle of the ammunition resulting in an explosion that killed him. Major Sharma's company held on to this position and the remnants withdrew only when almost completely surrounded. His inspiring example resulted in the enemy being delayed for six hours, thus gaining time for our reinforcements to get into position at Hum Hom to stem the tide of the enemy advance. His leadership, gallantry and tenacious defence were such that his men were inspired to fight the enemy by seven to one, six hours after this gallant officer had been killed. He has set an example of courage and qualities seldom equalled in the history of the Indian Army. His last message to the Brigade Headquarters a few moments before he was killed was, 'the enemy are only 50 yards from us. We are heavily outnumbered. We are under devastating fire. I shall not withdraw an inch but will fight to the last man and the last round.'	— Gazetteof	India	Notification:	2	Pres/50,	21.6.50As we walked further around the National War Memorial, we noticed another significant feature known as Amar Jawan Jyoti, which honours the sacrifice of Indian soldiers. As its name suggests, it symbolises an eternal11 flame that burns continuously, day and night, as a tribute to the brave soldiers who gave their lives for the nation. Earlier, the Amar Jawan Jyoti with a rifle and helmet displayed above it was located beneath the arch of India Gate. It was first lit in January 1972 to honour India’s victory in the 1971 Indo-Pakistan War.Today, the National War Memorial includes the Amar Jawan Jyoti at the central obelisk, which is surrounded by four circles known as Chakras. During the memorial’s inauguration, the Prime Minister lit a new flame to remember the fallen 11. eternal: lasting forever soldiers. Later, the flame that had been burning at India Gate 12. ceremonially: in a formal was ceremonially12 merged with the new one at the National and respectful way War Memorial.The India BookmarkThe Rezang La Memorial in Ladakh honours soldiers who fought at an altitude of over 5,000 metres during the 1962 war. The extreme cold and thin air made the battle as challenging as the enemy itself.The obelisk13 stands tall, surrounded by wreaths14 placed in respect for the martyrs. The monument’s lighting has been carefully designed so that the surroundings look different as the daylight shifts from morning to evening. The memorial 13. obelisk: a tall, upright stone is truly an impressive and moving place that filled me with structure with a pointed top pride for my country. The interminable15 flame seemed to 14. wreaths: rings of flowers placed to honour the dead represent the everlasting stories of bravery and sacrifice of 15. interminable — endless our soldiers.Our teacher also described the meaning and importance of the different Chakras at the memorial. I found this information very interesting, so I wanted to share it with you.The National War Memorial is designed with four circular layers arranged around a 15-metrehigh central obelisk that holds the eternal flame known as Amar Jawan Jyoti. These circles are called Amar Chakra, Veerta Chakra, Tyag Chakra, and Raksha Chakra. The memorial also features bronze and stone murals16 along with informative graphic panels, making it both educational and inspiring.The innermost circle, called Amar Chakra, is also known as the Circle of Immortality17. At its centre stands the obelisk with the eternal flame. This flame symbolises that the courage and spirit of the soldiers who sacrificed their lives will live forever, and that the nation will always remember their bravery.	16. murals: large pictures The second circle, known as Veerta Chakra or the Circle of 	painted or carved on wallsBravery, contains a covered gallery displaying six bronze 17. immortality: the state of being remembered forever murals. These artworks portray important battle scenes and heroic actions performed by members of the Indian Armed Forces.The third circle is the Tyag Chakra, also called the Circle of Sacrifice. Its circular walls of honour represent the ancient military formation known as the Chakravyuh. These walls are lined with granite tablets and each granite tablet at the memorial is dedicated to a soldier who lost their life in service of independent India. The names of these heroes are engraved in shining golden letters so that their bravery will always be remembered.The outermost ring of the memorial is known as the Raksha Chakra, or the Circle of Protection. In this section, rows of trees symbolise the protection and security provided to the nation’s citizens. Each tree represents the soldiers who safeguard the country and preserve its territorial unity.We spent the entire day exploring the National War Memorial and the surrounding area along Kartavyapath, which is known for its beautiful lawns and impressive buildings. The atmosphere of the place was calm and dignified18, reminding us of the strength and achievements of a free nation. The surroundings created a deeply moving and inspiring experience, and I felt a strong connection with the memorial. 18. dignified: calm, serious The visit taught me many things. At times, the discussions during and worthy of respect the tour were so touching that they brought tears to my eyes. Thinking about the destruction and loss caused by wars was painful. At the same time, the experience inspired me to live in a way that honours the courage and sacrifices of the brave soldiers who protected our country.You can learn more about the National War Memorial by visiting this website:https://www.mygov.in. Feel free to share the link with your friends so they can also explore more about it.We arrived back in Bengaluru yesterday. I ended up writing such a long letter because I was eager to share this memorable and inspiring experience with you.Please convey my greetings to your parents. I really miss the delicious chhole bhature your mom makes—you know how much I enjoy good food! Also, say hello to Amit for me.Your friend, ApoorvaLearning Through	Ask an ex-serviceman or defence personnel:memorial mean to soldiers and their families?	 What does a war InteractionWhere is the National War Memorial located?Mumbai 	(b) Delhi 	(c) Kolkata 	(d) ChennaiHow is the structure of the memorial designed?In a square form 	(b) In concentric circles 	(c) In a straight line 	(d) In a triangle  	3. The memorial honours soldiers who died after ______. 	(a) 1857 	(b) independence 	(c) 1900 	(d) partition 	4. What is inscribed on the walls of the memorial? 	(a) Poems 	(b) Names of soldiers (c) Historical dates only (d) Speeches  5. What does the eternal flame symbolize? 	(a) Power 	  	(b) Wealth 	(c) Sacrifice and remembrance   	(d) VictoryComplete the sentences 	/At the centre of the memorial stands a tall __________.The innermost circle of the memorial is called the __________________.The construction of the National War Memorial began in __________ and it was inaugurated in ____________ by ____________________.The Rakshak Chakra is lined with __________________________.India's highest gallantry award during wartime is the_______________ and _________________ was its first recipientAnswer the following questions with reference  to the context.“The trip turned out to be very informative, and I learned many fascinating things that I would love to share with you.”Who is the speaker of these lines?Which trip is being referred to here?Why did the speaker find the trip informative?What does this line tell you about the speaker’s feelings after the visit?“It covers nearly 40 acres of land close to the  	 famous India Gate in New Delhi.”  What does ‘it’ refer to?Who is the writer addressing?What does this line tell us about its size?What is the national importance of ‘it’?Answer the following questions 	/Where is the National War Memorial located? What is its significance?What are the different sections of the memorial? Name them.Describe the Amar Chakra and its importance.What does the Eternal Flame at the memorial represent?What is the significance of the names inscribed on the walls of the memorial?Write a short note on the bravery of Major Somnath Sharma.Put on Your Thinking Caps /Complete the table given below.Name of the War HeroContribution and achievementAward GivenMajor Somnath SharmaBattle of Badgam in 1947First Param Vir Chakra awarded posthumously Brig M. UsmanSam ManekshawLance Naik Albert Ekka	Dictionary & Vocabulary Skills	  	/Use a dictionary to find the following information for the word ‘everlasting’.Part of speech- _________________________________________________________Number of Syllables- ____________________________________________________A short meaning- _______________________________________________________Usage in a sentence- ____________________________________________________Find word associated with 'brave' by filling in the missing vowels in the boxes given below.Types of ConjunctionsCoordinating Conjunctions- These conjunctions join two equal parts of a sentence.  	 Common Coordinating conjunctions: and, but, or, so, yet  	 Examples :The students observed the memorial carefully and noted down important facts.The memorial is peaceful, but it reminds us of painful sacrifices.Subordinating Conjunctions- These join a main clause with a dependent clause.  	 Common Subordinating Conjunctions: because, although, when, while, if, since, as 	 Examples :The students felt emotional because they learned about the sacrifices of soldiers.Although the atmosphere was calm, it was deeply moving.Correlative Conjunctions- These conjunctions work in pairs to join similar elements. 	 Examples : either…or, neither…nor, not only…but also 	 Examples :The memorial honours not only soldiers but also their sacrifices.Neither the students nor the teacher spoke loudly at the site.The visit was either educational or emotional for every student.Underline the conjunction and write whether it is coordinating / subordinating / correlative.She wanted to speak, but she remained silent.The players practised hard because they wanted to win the match.Either you complete your work now or you will have to stay back after school.Although he was tired, he continued his journey.The teacher explained the concept clearly and gave many examples.If you work sincerely, you will achieve your goals.Neither the manager nor the employees were aware of the mistake.He ran fast so that he could catch the bus.While the others were playing, she was completing her homework.A moment when you felt proud of your countryOne responsibility every citizen should follow	Writing Skills	  	CG:2, C:2.2	Critical ThinkingYou have learned about the courage and sacrifices of the Armed Forces during times of war. However, their role is equally important during peaceful times.Write a letter to your friend explaining how the Armed Forces serve the nation during natural disasters such as floods and earthquakes, contribute to development activities like building roads and bridges in remote areas, and support society through welfare efforts such as medical camps and educational initiatives.CompetenciesBeyondCG:2, C:2.2	 Interconnected Learning with Hindi  	Critical ThinkingRead the following poem:	     Pushp ki Abhilasha	heg<he keâer DeefYeuee<ee	Chaah Nahi, Main Sur Bala Ke 	Ûeen veneR, ceQ megjyeeuee kesâGehanon Mein Goontha Jaaun, 	ienveeW ceW ietBLee peeTB, Chaah Nahi, Premi-Mala Mein 	Ûeen veneR, heÇsceer-ceeuee ceW Bandh Pyaari Ko Lalchaaun! 	efyebOe hÙeejer keâes ueueÛeeTB!	Chaah Nahi, Samraaton Ke Shav Par, 	Ûeen veneR, meceÇešeW kesâ Meke hej,	Hey Hari, Dala Jaaun, 	ns nefj, [euee peeTB,Chaah Nahi Devon Ke Sir Par 	Ûeen veneR, oskeeW kesâ efmej hej Chadhoon, Bhagya Par Ithlaun! What steps can you take as a responsible citizen?You are given a chance to speak in assembly on ‘Respect for Armed Forces’. What two key points would you include and why?	 Explore 	CG:2, C:2.2	Critical ThinkingScan & LearnScan the QR code to access Ask questions, clear yout the worksheet for this doubts, and learn more with chapter.AI.India’s security is maintained not only by the Army, Navy and Air Force, but also by several other forces. Find out about the different armed and security forces of India and write about any 5 of them.
